name: E2E pipeline: a Kubernetes rule scraped from a source is tagged to the kubernetes IaC dialect, routed to a container scanner, and the whole-tree audit flags a privileged-pod repo red while passing a compliant repo green — scrape to audit for the IaC axis
command:
printf '# K8s Security

## No privileged containers
Kubernetes pods must not run privileged containers.
' > k.md && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/extract-rules-from-url.sh" --source k.md --source-id k8s-sec --json > rules.json 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/classify-rule.sh" --title 'No privileged' --prose 'Kubernetes pods must not run privileged containers.' --json > c.json 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/route-rule.sh" --in c.json --json > r.json 2>/dev/null && node -e 'const c=require(process.cwd()+"/c.json"),r=require(process.cwd()+"/r.json");if(!c.applies_to.iac_dialects.includes("kubernetes"))process.exit(1);if(!r.enforced_by.some(x=>x.tool==="checkov"||x.tool==="kubescape"||x.tool==="trivy"))process.exit(1)' && mkdir -p bad && printf 'apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
spec:
  containers:
  - securityContext:
      privileged: true
' > bad/pod.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/composite-audit.sh" --root bad 2>b.txt; be=$?; mkdir -p ok && printf 'name: app
' > ok/config.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/composite-audit.sh" --root ok 2>o.txt; oe=$?; [ "$be" -eq 1 ] && grep -q 'status=red' b.txt && [ "$oe" -eq 0 ] && grep -q 'status=green' o.txt && echo pipeline-iac-url-to-audit 1>&2
